title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: document describes reproduce HoVerNet nuclei segmentation results underlie pre-computed features available imageFeatureTCGA. complete setup instructions, scripts, environment files maintained hovernethelp repository Hervé Pagès. HoVer-Net (Graham et al., 2019) performs simultaneous nuclei detection, segmentation, classification histopathology whole-slide images (WSI). pipeline run TCGA diagnostic images outputs (JSON, GeoJSON, H5AD, thumbnails) accessible via imageFeatureTCGA.

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: Running HoVerNet scratch requires GPU resources: Processing time approximately 76–81 minutes per image single GPU node. 7-node mini-cluster (described ) achieves full TCGA dataset 15–25 hours. need pre-computed results, skip imageFeatureTCGA workflow vignette — GPU required access stored features.

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: instructions follow hovernethelp setup guide.

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: NVIDIA A100: NVIDIA L40S: Verify installation:
code: sudo apt-get install nvidia-linux-grid-535 sudo apt-get install nvidia-driver-550 nvidia-smi

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: environment.yml pins key packages: Python 3.12.3 PyTorch 2.5.1 opencv-python, scikit-image, scikit-learn, scipy, pandas openslide whole-slide image reading
code: # Install Miniconda wget https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh bash Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh conda init bash && source ~/.bashrc  # Clone HoVer-Net git clone https://github.com/vqdang/hover_net cd hover_net  # Create conda environment from hovernethelp wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hpages/hovernethelp/main/environment.yml conda env create -f environment.yml conda activate hovernet

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: pannuke_type model (TF2→PyTorch conversion) downloaded gdown: See hovernethelp setup file exact model ID path configuration.
code: pip install gdown gdown <model_gdrive_id> -O pannuke_type_tf2pytorch.tar tar -xf pannuke_type_tf2pytorch.tar

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: Images downloaded using GenomicDataCommons Bioconductor package. imageTCGA Shiny app generates query code interactively (see main workflow vignette).
code: # Example: download a single OV diagnostic image library(GenomicDataCommons)  q <- files() |>     filter(~ cases.project.project_id == "TCGA-OV" &                data_type == "Slide Image" &                experimental_strategy == "Diagnostic Slide") |>     results(size = 1)  gdcdata(q$file_id, destination_dir = "tcga_images/")

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: Key parameters:
code: python run_infer.py \   --gpu='0' \   --nr_types=6 \   --type_info_path=type_info.json \   --batch_size=12 \   --model_mode=original \   --model_path=pannuke_type_tf2pytorch/hovernet_fast_pannuke_type_tf2pytorch.tar \   --nr_inference_workers=1 \   --nr_post_proc_workers=3 \   wsi \   --input_dir=tcga_images/ \   --output_dir=hovernet_output/ \   --save_thumb \   --save_mask

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: infer_batch2.sh script automates full pipeline: Reads manifest TCGA image IDs process Downloads SVS file via GenomicDataCommons (rsync retry, 10 attempts 5-minute intervals) Runs HoVerNet inference validates JSON output Pushes results central storage node (hoverboss) Updates manifest success/failure status timestamps
code: # Usage bash infer_batch2.sh manifest.tsv

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: input SVS image, HoVerNet produces: files exactly imageFeatureTCGA imports — see importHoVerNet() load SpatialExperiment.

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
text: hovernethelp minicluster setup describes 7-node architecture: Worker nodes (hovernet1–4, hovernet6–7, kakapo1): GPU nodes running inference Central node (hoverboss): 4 CPUs, 779 GB storage, collects results Workers sync completed JSON/H5AD files hoverboss via cron every 15 minutes. worker processes non-overlapping range TCGA image list. Total throughput: 100+ images/day, completing full TCGA dataset 15–25 hours.

title: Reproducing HoVerNet Nuclei Segmentation on TCGA Images
Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: workflow demonstrates complete analysis pipeline histopathology image data Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA), using suite Bioconductor packages developed around imageTCGA ecosystem: TCGA image database contains ~11,765 diagnostic whole-slide images (WSI) ~9,640 patients across many cancer types. Pre-computed features available 54,253 files: 33,177 HoVerNet (nuclei segmentation/ classification) 21,076 Prov-GigaPath (slide- tile-level deep learning embeddings).

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: packages available GitHub active development submitted Bioconductor. Install follows:
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager")  # Bioconductor dependencies BiocManager::install(c(     "SpatialExperiment", "SingleCellExperiment", "MultiAssayExperiment",     "BiocFileCache", "BiocIO", "BumpyMatrix", "GenomicDataCommons" ))  # imageTCGA ecosystem (GitHub) BiocManager::install(c(     "billila/imageTCGA",     "waldronlab/imageFeatureTCGA",     "waldronlab/imageTCGAutils",     "waldronlab/HistoImagePlot" ))

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
code: library(imageTCGA) library(imageFeatureTCGA) library(imageTCGAutils) library(HistoImagePlot) library(MultiAssayExperiment) library(SpatialExperiment) library(dplyr)

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: imageTCGA Shiny application provides interactive interface TCGA diagnostic image database. allows filtering cancer type, clinical parameters (histological type, grade, stage), patient geography, generates ready--run GenomicDataCommons download code. app displays: Dot plots image counts stratified clinical variables gynaecological tumours (BRCA, OV, UCS, UCEC). Interactive maps showing origin institution image country, US-state, centre level. code generation panel produces GenomicDataCommons query corresponding current filter selection. identified case(s) interest, copy generated code use next step.
code: # Launch the Shiny app imageTCGA::run_app()

what: Step 2: Retrieving Pre-computed Features with imageFeatureTCGA
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: imageFeatureTCGA provides access catalogue 54,253 pre-computed feature files hosted Waldron Lab server. Two computational pipelines covered: HoVerNet – nuclei detection, segmentation, classification single-nucleus resolution. Output formats: JSON (coordinates + contours), GeoJSON, H5AD (features), PNG thumbnails. Prov-GigaPath – vision foundation model embeddings slide level (one vector per WSI) tile level (one vector per 256 × 256 px tile).

previous_headings: Step 2: Retrieving Pre-computed Features with imageFeatureTCGA
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: catalogue columns include file_id, cases.submitter_id (TCGA barcode), cancer_type, pipeline, data_type, remote url.
code: # Inspect the full data catalogue cat <- imageFeatureTCGA::imageCatalogue() head(cat) # Filter to HoVerNet H5AD files for ovarian cancer ov_h5ad <- cat |>     dplyr::filter(cancer_type == "OV",                   pipeline == "HoVerNet",                   data_type == "h5ad")  nrow(ov_h5ad) head(ov_h5ad[, c("cases.submitter_id", "pipeline", "data_type")])

previous_headings: Step 2: Retrieving Pre-computed Features with imageFeatureTCGA
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: HoVerNet outputs imported SpatialExperiment objects row nucleus spatial coordinates reflect centroid slide. resulting SpatialExperiment contains: - assay("X") – per-nucleus feature matrix (intensity statistics, morphology) - spatialCoords() – centroid (x, y) slide pixel coordinates - colData() – cell type label, nucleus area, QC flags
code: # Import HoVerNet H5AD data for a single slide spe_hover <- imageFeatureTCGA::importHoVerNet(     file_id  = ov_h5ad$file_id[1],     format   = "h5ad" ) spe_hover # Import HoVerNet JSON (includes contours) for the same slide spe_json <- imageFeatureTCGA::importHoVerNet(     file_id  = ov_h5ad$file_id[1],     format   = "json" )

previous_headings: Step 2: Retrieving Pre-computed Features with imageFeatureTCGA
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: column 256 × 256 px tile; 1,536-dimensional embedding vector Prov-GigaPath stored assay("embeddings").
code: # Filter catalogue to tile-level Prov-GigaPath files for one OV slide pg_tiles <- cat |>     dplyr::filter(cases.submitter_id == ov_h5ad$cases.submitter_id[1],                   pipeline == "ProvGigaPath",                   data_type == "tile_embeddings")  spe_tiles <- imageFeatureTCGA::importProvGigaPath(     file_id  = pg_tiles$file_id[1],     level    = "tile" ) spe_tiles

previous_headings: Step 2: Retrieving Pre-computed Features with imageFeatureTCGA
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: assays can linked TCGA clinical metadata single MultiAssayExperiment:
code: mae <- MultiAssayExperiment::MultiAssayExperiment(     experiments = list(         HoVerNet    = spe_hover,         GigaPath    = spe_tiles     ) ) mae

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: imageTCGAutils provides utility functions dimensionality reduction spatial autocorrelation tile- nucleus-level data.

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
code: # Run PCA on Prov-GigaPath tile embeddings and store result in reducedDim spe_tiles <- imageTCGAutils::runTilePCA(spe_tiles, ncomponents = 30)  # Visualise first two PCs coloured by tissue region imageTCGAutils::plotTileReducedDim(spe_tiles, dimred = "PCA",                                     colour_by = "tissue_class")

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: Moran’s Geary’s C quantify whether feature (e.g. proportion tumour nuclei tile) spatially clustered, dispersed, random. Local Moran’s (LISA) identifies individual tiles drive global clustering.
code: # Compute global Moran's I for the tumour-nucleus proportion per tile mi <- imageTCGAutils::globalMoransI(     spe   = spe_tiles,     assay = "embeddings",     feat  = "tumour_prop" ) mi  # Local Moran's I (LISA) — returns a SpatialExperiment with LISA scores spe_tiles <- imageTCGAutils::localMoransI(spe_tiles, feat = "tumour_prop")  # Geary's C gc <- imageTCGAutils::globalGearysC(spe_tiles, feat = "tumour_prop") gc

what: Matching HoVerNet nuclei to Prov-GigaPath tiles
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
code: # Assign each nucleus (spe_hover) to its containing tile (spe_tiles) spe_hover <- imageTCGAutils::matchNucleiToTiles(     nuclei = spe_hover,     tiles  = spe_tiles ) # colData(spe_hover)$tile_id now contains the parent tile identifier

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: HistoImagePlot renders side--side panels tissue thumbnail nucleus segmentation overlay, useful quality control illustrating findings.

title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
text: Supported colour palettes can passed via palette =. thumbnail fetched catalogue cached automatically BiocFileCache.
code: # Import HoVerNet JSON for the slide (includes nucleus contours) spe_json <- HistoImagePlot::importHoVerNetJSON(     file_id = ov_h5ad$file_id[1] )  # Plot thumbnail next to coloured segmentation map HistoImagePlot::plotHistoImage(     spe        = spe_json,     colour_by  = "cell_type",     point_size = 0.4,     title      = paste("OV –", ov_h5ad$cases.submitter_id[1]) )

what: Overlay on a region of interest
title: Histopathology Image Analysis with TCGA Data: an end-to-end workflow
code: # Restrict to a 2000 × 2000 px region starting at (5000, 3000) HistoImagePlot::plotHistoImage(     spe       = spe_json,     colour_by = "cell_type",     xlim      = c(5000, 7000),     ylim      = c(3000, 5000) )
